META.reasoning: Two of five agents are explicitly bearish (swing_mean_reversion at 68 confidence, swing_catalyst_news at 78 confidence); three are neutral (swing_trend_momentum at 82, swing_breakout at 22, swing_macro_context at 38). On raw vote count this looks like a 3-neutral / 2-bearish split, but the neutral calls are context-conditional — every neutral agent is neutral because no entry exists *right now* at $611-613, not because they are bullish. All five agents converge on the same picture: META suffered a post-earnings institutional distribution collapse (-7.9% on April 30, 3.34x average volume), the prior $675-677 breakout thesis is fully invalidated, and price is below ALL EMAs on daily and hourly. The three neutrals are waiting for a dead-cat bounce to $618-630 before acting; the two bearish agents plan to short that same bounce. This is functional agreement dressed as disagreement: when the bounce materializes, 4-of-5 agents effectively shift to bearish. Macro context is risk-on (S&P/Nasdaq at record highs) but swing_macro_context applies a stock-specific veto: the capex shock narrative is decoupled from the broad market tailwind and the asymmetric R/R at current levels is negative (<1:1 for longs, <2:1 for shorts). A short setup only qualifies once price bounces to the $626-630 resistance cluster (Fib 38.2%, broken prior support), where the R/R improves to approximately 3:1 (entry ~$628, target ~$583, stop ~$643). Wiki memory reinforces caution: the only recent comparable setup — EMA pullback Fib dip-buy on NVDA — produced a -$264.65 loss (0% win rate on this setup-type in the last 30 days), confirming that 'oversold AI name post-catalyst' longs are a losing pattern. META-specific trade history shows no open position and two prior closed/abandoned longs at $673-677 both resulted in losses — no long-bias recency bias to overcome. Recommended action: no trade until a bounce to $626-630 is confirmed intraday; then short with entry ~$628, target ~$585 (Fib 61.8%), stop ~$643. If price breaks below $600 support on volume without a bounce, re-evaluate for a momentum continuation short entry below that level.
META.key_conflicts: The primary conflict is swing_trend_momentum (neutral, 82 confidence) vs swing_mean_reversion and swing_catalyst_news (both bearish). Trend_momentum is neutral because its strategy rules require price to be ABOVE EMAs with accelerating ROC — those conditions are absent, so it outputs 'no setup' rather than a directional call. Mean_reversion is bearish because it reads the same oversold conditions as a dead-cat bounce opportunity to fade, not a dip to buy. This is the classic 'trend_momentum bearish + mean_reversion bearish = trend extended, NOT counter-trend opportunity' pattern from the prompt: both are pointing the same direction by different paths. The genuine conflict is swing_macro_context (neutral): it acknowledges the broad macro is risk-on but refuses to call a short until asymmetric R/R is present (requires the bounce). This is a timing disagreement, not a directional one — macro_context agrees the direction is bearish but vetoes a trade at current prices on R/R grounds. Swing_breakout is the odd signal: confidence 22 (lowest of all agents), outputs neutral with entry/target/stop all set to $611.91 (placeholder) — effectively abstaining because no coiled-spring setup exists. Its low confidence prevents it from distorting the synthesis. The only genuine directional disagreement is swing_catalyst_news placing the short entry at $630 vs swing_mean_reversion at $619 — a $11 spread on entry. These cluster tightly enough (both within the $618-633 resistance band) to use the median ($624-625) or wait for a confirmed intraday reversal candle in that zone before shorting.
META.condition: NO TRADE until price bounces to $626-630 intraday and shows a confirmed rejection candle (bearish engulfing or pin bar on hourly). If price drops straight through $600 support on volume without a bounce, reassess for a continuation short below $597.
